Cholesterol in LDL [Mass/volume] in Serum or Plasma by Direct assay
Cholesterol in HDL [Mass/volume] in Serum or Plasma

tr.th: Conclusion and Recommendations based on this report and previous findings known to us

tr.td: The patient shows elevated HbA1c, indicating impaired glucose metabolism (prediabetes/early diabetes). Lipid profile reveals high total cholesterol and LDL, increasing cardiovascular risk. Chloride is mildly elevated, but renal function and electrolytes are otherwise normal. Hematological parameters are within normal limits, except for a high platelet distribution width, which may suggest platelet activation or variability. Recommend further evaluation and management of dyslipidemia and glycemic control.

display.value: Disorder of kidney due to diabetes mellitus

text.div: Provider Organization: KfH-Nierenzentrum am Klinikum Freising
